rationale: Review 1 surfaces a real, diff-confirmable naming issue Review 2 explicitly denies: the field `unipotent_radical_trivial : True` (and the vacuous `unipotentRadical_trivial'` lemma) whose names assert content the `True` type doesn't carry, plus a defensible internal-consistency push toward `Is`-prefixed classes. Its only weak finding (AlgPoints/roadmap) still has a grounded fallback, so acting on Review 1 yields a more honest, consistent PR than Review 2's blanket approve.
